session.user:
I need you to update all remaining `FakeCodespace()` calls (with NO arguments) across the codebase to pass explicit arguments. The pattern is:
Replace: `FakeCodespace()` With: `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=0, repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="fake-gh-name")`
Also update partial-argument calls to include ALL three arguments: - `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=42)` → `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=42, repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="fake-gh-name")` - `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=1)` → `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=1, repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="fake-gh-name")` - `FakeCodespace(created_codespace_name="gh-custom-abc")` → `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=0, repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="gh-custom-abc")` - `FakeCodespace(repo_id=99999)` → `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=0, repo_id=99999, created_codespace_name="fake-gh-name")` - `FakeCodespace(repo_id=42)` → `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=0, repo_id=42, created_codespace_name="fake-gh-name")` - `FakeCodespace(repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="gh-mybox-abc")` → `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=0, repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="gh-mybox-abc")` - `FakeCodespace(created_codespace_name="gh-mybox-abc")` → `FakeCodespace(run_exit_code=0, repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="gh-mybox-abc")`
For multi-line calls like: ```python FakeCodespace( run_exit_code=1, created_codespace_name="gh-mybox-abc", ) ``` Add the missing `repo_id=12345` parameter.
Files that ALREADY have been updated (skip these): - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/codespace/fake.py` (the class itself, already done) - `src/erk/core/context.py` (both calls already done) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/factories.py` (already done) - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py` (already done) - The FIRST `FakeCodespace()` call in `tests/unit/gateways/codespace/test_fake_codespace.py` at the original line 13 (now updated)
Files that still need updating: - `tests/unit/gateways/codespace/test_fake_codespace.py` (remaining ~15 calls) - `tests/unit/cli/commands/codespace/run/objective/test_plan_cmd.py` (~7 calls) - `tests/unit/cli/commands/codespace/test_setup_cmd.py` (~9 calls) - `tests/unit/cli/commands/codespace/test_connect_cmd.py` (~14 calls)
Read each file first, then make the edits. Use the Edit tool. For files with many `FakeCodespace()` no-arg calls, you can use `replace_all=true` for the simple replacement pattern if it's the only pattern in the file.
IMPORTANT: Maintain proper indentation. The replacement should fit the surrounding code style. When a call is on a single line and adding all three params would make it too long, format it multi-line: ```python codespace = FakeCodespace( run_exit_code=0, repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="fake-gh-name" ) ```
For the test files, keep multi-line format for readability since the line would be long.
session.assistant: I'll update all the remaining `FakeCodespace()` calls across the codebase. Let me start by reading the files that still need updating.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Tests for FakeCodespace implementation.""" 2→ 3→import pytest 4→ 5→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace.fake import FakeCodespace, SSHCall 6→ 7→ 8→class TestFakeCodespaceRunSSHCommand: 9→ """Tests for FakeCodespace run_ssh_command tracking functionality.""" 10→ 11→ def test_run_tracks_calls(self) -> None: 12→ """run_ssh_command() calls are tracked in ssh_calls property.""" 13→ codespace = FakeCodespace( 14→ run_exit_code=0, repo_id=12345, created_codespace_name="fake-gh-name" 15→ ) 16→ codespace.run_ssh_command("cs-abc123", "echo hello") 17→ codespace.run_ssh_command("cs-def456", "pwd") 18→ 19→ assert len(codespace.ssh_calls) == 2 20→ assert codespace.ssh_calls[0] == SSHCall( 21→ gh_name="cs-abc123", remote_command="echo hello", interactive=False 22→ ) 23→ assert codespace.ssh_calls[1] == SSHCall( 24→ gh_name="cs-def456", remote_command="pwd", interactive=False 25→ ) 26→ 27→ def test_last_call_returns_most_recent(self) -> None: 28→ """last_call returns most recent SSH call.""" 29→ codespace = FakeCodespace() 30→ codespace.run_ssh_command("cs-first", "cmd1")
... [145 lines omitted] ...
69→ def test_failure_mode_still_tracks_calls(self) -> None: 70→ """Failure mode still tracks SSH calls."""

1→"""Unit tests for codespace run objective plan command.""" 2→ 3→from datetime import datetime 4→ 5→from click.testing import CliRunner 6→ 7→from erk.cli.cli import cli 8→from erk.core.context import context_for_test 9→from erk_shared.context.types import LoadedConfig 10→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace.fake import FakeCodespace 11→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace_registry.abc import RegisteredCodespace 12→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace_registry.fake import FakeCodespaceRegistry 13→ 14→ 15→def _make_codespace(name: str) -> RegisteredCodespace: 16→ return RegisteredCodespace( 17→ name=name, 18→ gh_name=f"user-{name}-abc123", 19→ created_at=datetime(2026, 1, 20, 8, 0, 0), 20→ ) 21→ 22→ 23→def test_run_plan_starts_codespace_and_runs_command() -> None: 24→ """run objective plan starts the codespace and runs the command.""" 25→ runner = CliRunner() 26→ 27→ cs = _make_codespace("mybox") 28→ fake_codespace = FakeCodespace() 29→ codespace_registry = FakeCodespaceRegistry(codespaces=[cs], default_codespace="mybox") 30→ ctx = context_for_test(codespace=fake_codespace, codespace_registry=codespace_registry)
... [199 lines omitted] ...
36→ catch_exceptions=False, 70→ catch_exceptions=False, 89→ catch_exceptions=False, 109→ catch_exceptions=False, 134→ catch_exceptions=False, 160→ catch_exceptions=False, 191→ catch_exceptions=False, 217→ catch_exceptions=False,

1→"""Unit tests for codespace setup command.""" 2→ 3→from datetime import datetime 4→from pathlib import Path 5→ 6→from click.testing import CliRunner 7→ 8→from erk.cli.cli import cli 9→from erk.cli.commands.codespace.setup_cmd import DEFAULT_MACHINE_TYPE 10→from erk.core.context import context_for_test 11→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace.fake import FakeCodespace 12→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace_registry.abc import RegisteredCodespace 13→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace_registry.fake import FakeCodespaceRegistry 14→from erk_shared.gateway.erk_installation.fake import FakeErkInstallation 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import RepoInfo 16→ 17→TEST_REPO_INFO = RepoInfo(owner="testorg", name="testrepo") 18→ 19→ 20→def test_setup_shows_error_when_name_exists() -> None: 21→ """setup command shows error if codespace with same name exists.""" 22→ runner = CliRunner() 23→ 24→ cs = RegisteredCodespace( 25→ name="mybox", gh_name="user-mybox-abc", created_at=datetime(2026, 1, 20, 8, 0, 0) 26→ ) 27→ codespace_registry = FakeCodespaceRegistry(codespaces=[cs]) 28→ ctx = context_for_test(codespace_registry=codespace_registry) 29→ 30→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "setup", "mybox"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False)
... [217 lines omitted] ...
51→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "setup"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False) 72→ # No repo info → error at _resolve_owner_repo 73→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "setup"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=True) 95→ cli, ["codespace", "setup", "custom-name"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False 103→def test_setup_errors_without_repo_info_or_repo_flag() -> None: 104→ """setup command errors when no repo_info and no --repo flag provided.""" 110→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "setup", "mybox"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=True) 134→ catch_exceptions=False, 155→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "setup", "mybox"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False) 184→ catch_exceptions=False, 210→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "setup", "mybox"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False) 220→def test_setup_shows_retry_hint_on_login_failure(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 237→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "setup", "mybox"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False) 240→ assert "Claude login may have failed or was cancelled" in result.output

1→"""Unit tests for codespace connect command.""" 2→ 3→from datetime import datetime 4→from pathlib import Path 5→ 6→from click.testing import CliRunner 7→ 8→from erk.cli.cli import cli 9→from erk.core.context import context_for_test 10→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 11→from erk_shared.context.types import LoadedConfig 12→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace.fake import FakeCodespace 13→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace_registry.abc import RegisteredCodespace 14→from erk_shared.gateway.codespace_registry.fake import FakeCodespaceRegistry 15→from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit 16→ 17→ 18→def test_connect_shows_error_when_no_codespaces() -> None: 19→ """connect command shows error when no codespaces are registered.""" 20→ runner = CliRunner() 21→ 22→ codespace_registry = FakeCodespaceRegistry() 23→ ctx = context_for_test(codespace_registry=codespace_registry) 24→ 25→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "connect"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False) 26→ 27→ assert result.exit_code == 1 28→ assert "No default codespace set" in result.output 29→ assert "erk codespace setup" in result.output 30→
... [371 lines omitted] ...
32→def test_connect_shows_error_when_named_codespace_not_found() -> None: 33→ """connect command shows error when specified codespace doesn't exist.""" 40→ cli, ["codespace", "connect", "nonexistent"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False 48→def test_connect_shows_error_when_default_not_found() -> None: 49→ """connect command shows error when default codespace no longer exists.""" 64→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["codespace", "connect"], obj=ctx, catch_exceptions=False) 211→def test_connect_env_invalid_format_errors() -> None: 212→ """connect --env with invalid format (no =) shows error."""
